images.caption:
Figure 2:Optimizer-dependent spectral scaling across token-frequency regimes.
Soft spectral rank (top) and hard spectral rank (bottom) are shown as functions
of FFN hidden width for HEAD, MID, and TAIL tokens in GPT-2 160M. AdamW exhibits persistent positive hard–soft asymmetry, indicating that
added width contributes mostly to diffuse spectral capacity rather than
dominant-mode capacity. Muon and NorMuon reduce this asymmetry to near zero for MID and TAIL tokens,
whereas low-rank Dion (r=1/16r=1/16) retains AdamW-like asymmetry despite
orthonormalized updates.

Figure 3:Extended AdamW training weakens hard-rank scaling in GPT-2
160M. Hard-rank scaling (βhard\beta_{\mathrm{hard}}) exhibits diminishing return for TAIL token scaling. Soft-rank scaling remains comparatively stable,
leading to increasing hard–soft asymmetry at 12K (see Table3).

Figure 5:Optimizer-dependent TAIL spectral scaling persists at 350M
scale. Soft spectral rank (left) and hard spectral rank (right) are shown
across a four-point FFN-width sweep for TAIL tokens in GPT-2 350M. Muon and
NorMuon maintain stronger hard-rank scaling than AdamW, while low-rank
Dion(1/161/16) remains in a lower hard-capacity (βhard\beta_{\mathrm{hard}}), while their soft-rank scaling incurred less degradation, indicating that
low update rank primarily limits dominant-mode hard capacity.

Figure 6:Optimizer-induced shifts in spectral-scaling exceed attention-rank shifts in GPT-2 160M.
We compare the optimizer-induced spectral scaling shift for AdamW in the original1212-head
architecture,Δ​βopt⋆\Delta\beta_{\mathrm{opt}}^{\star}(shown as red dashed lines), and the spectral scaling shift induced by architectural
intervention, increasing the attention ranks at fixed total parameter
count, across each optimizer (shown as bars). Optimizer-induced gains exceed attention-rank shifts in55of66frequency regimes (marked with★\bigstar), across2828of3030scaling shifts; the only exceptions occur in HEAD
hard-rank scaling.

Figure 9:Depth profiles of layer-wise scaling exponents for GPT-2 160M. Each

header: Appendix EHard-Rank Dynamics Under Extended AdamW Training

images.caption:
Figure 10:Hard-rank dynamics for AdamW extended training with GPT-2
160M. Post-activation hard ranks are shown across 12K training steps for
representative FFN widths (1×1\times,2×2\times,4×4\times,8×8\times).
In HEAD regime, all widths rise early and then
decline toward similar values. In MID regimes, width ordering is partially violated
at the end of training—8×8\timestrajectory falling below the4×4\timestrajectory. In TAIL regimes, the breakdown isstrongest:the8×8\timestrajectory peaks early, drops sharply after roughly 7K steps, falls below
the2×2\timestrajectory around 8K, and remains far below the4×4\timestrajectory at 12K.
